maxContractTerm.value: Bifurcated rule. (a) Operating-services contract with a commercial transportation company (district contracts with the company to provide transportation): No statutory maximum found - Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 is silent on contract term/duration. (b) Contract for the use, acquisition, or lease with option to purchase of a school bus (the district leases/acquires the vehicle itself): not less than 2 years and not more than 10 years - Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.009(j) provides: 'A contract under this section may have any lawful term of not less than two or more than 10 years.' Sec. 34.009(c) treats contracts that reserve the board's right to terminate at the expiration of each budget period as a commitment of current revenues only (annual-appropriation/non-substitution structure). The generally applicable school-district procurement statute (Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 44.031) does not impose a term cap for services contracts.
maxContractTerm.citation: Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 (silent as to term) (https://statutes.capitol.texas.gov/Docs/ED/htm/ED.34.htm); Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.009(j) (2-10 year cap for school-bus use/acquisition/lease contracts); Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.009(c) (budget-period termination right = current-revenues commitment); Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 44.031 (general procurement; silent on term) (https://statutes.capitol.texas.gov/Docs/ED/htm/ED.44.htm).

rebidRequired.label: Conditional
rebidRequired.rule: Texas does not require rebidding at the simple expiration of a transportation contract by force of a transportation-specific statute. Rebidding is triggered by the general school-district procurement statute, Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 44.031(a), which (as amended by Acts 2025, 89th Leg., R.S., S.B. 1173, eff. Sept. 1, 2025) requires that 'all school district contracts for the purchase of goods and services, except contracts for the purchase of produce or vehicle fuel, valued at $100,000 or more in the aggregate for each 12-month period shall be made by the method ... that provides the best value for the district,' choosing from (1) competitive bidding for services other than construction services, (2) competitive sealed proposals for services other than construction services, (3) a request for proposals for services other than construction services, (4) an interlocal contract, (5) the methods under Government Code Ch. 2269 for construction services, (6) a reverse auction procedure, or (7) formation of a political subdivision corporation. The $100,000 threshold reflects S.B. 1173's amendment from $50,000; the transition rule limits the new threshold to purchases made on or after Sept. 1, 2025. For school bus PURCHASE OR LEASE contracts, Sec. 44.031(l) requires competitive bidding when the contract is valued at $20,000 or more. At term-end, if the district will continue contracting for transportation at a value at or above the applicable threshold, it must use one of the listed best-value methods - but those methods include interlocal contracts and cooperative purchasing, so a strict re-bid is not the only path. Sec. 34.009(e) separately confirms that the competitive bidding requirements of Subchapter B, Chapter 44 apply to a contract under Sec. 34.009.
rebidRequired.citation: Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 44.031(a), (l) (https://statutes.capitol.texas.gov/Docs/ED/htm/ED.44.htm); Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.009(e) (https://statutes.capitol.texas.gov/Docs/ED/htm/ED.34.htm); Acts 2025, 89th Leg., R.S., S.B. 1173 (raising threshold to $100,000 eff. 9/1/2025) (https://capitol.texas.gov/tlodocs/89R/billtext/html/SB01173F.htm).

renewalMechanics.value: No express statutory renewal-without-rebid mechanism in Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 (commercial-transportation contracts). Sec. 34.009 (school-bus use/acquisition/lease) sets a 2-10 year window (Sec. 34.009(j)) and addresses termination/budget-period mechanics (Sec. 34.009(c): contracts reserving the board's right to terminate at the expiration of each budget period are a commitment of current revenues only; Sec. 34.009(d): 'Termination penalties may not be included in any contract under this section'). The general procurement statute, Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 44.031, lists permissible procurement methods but does not codify renewal/extension procedures, CPI/fuel escalators, automatic rollover, or evergreen renewal. As a matter of district practice (recorded here as practice, not statute), Texas school district transportation services RFPs are commonly structured as a base term (commonly 5 years) plus one or more renewal options (commonly an additional 5 years) exercised by mutual written agreement and board approval; these renewal-option mechanisms are creatures of the contract and the district's board policy, not of state statute. No interlocal/cooperative-purchasing path can convert a stale contract into a fresh procurement absent a new best-value determination under Sec. 44.031.
renewalMechanics.citation: Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 (no renewal mechanics) (https://statutes.capitol.texas.gov/Docs/ED/htm/ED.34.htm); Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.009(c), (d), (j); Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 44.031 (general procurement, no renewal mechanics specified) (https://statutes.capitol.texas.gov/Docs/ED/htm/ED.44.htm).

finalFields.statutoryCitation: Primary: Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 (Contract with Transit Authority, Commercial Transportation Company, or Juvenile Board) (https://statutes.capitol.texas.gov/Docs/ED/htm/ED.34.htm); Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.009 (Contracts for Use, Acquisition, or Lease of School Bus), particularly subsections (c), (d), (e), and (j); Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 44.031 (Purchasing Contracts), particularly subsections (a), (b), and (l) (https://statutes.capitol.texas.gov/Docs/ED/htm/ED.44.htm). Definitional/applicability: Tex. Educ. Code Ch. 11 (school districts) and Ch. 34 (Transportation). Recent amendment: Acts 2025, 89th Leg., R.S., S.B. 1173 (filed without governor's signature 5/29/2025; eff. Sept. 1, 2025), raising the Sec. 44.031(a) threshold from $50,000 to $100,000 (https://capitol.texas.gov/tlodocs/89R/billtext/html/SB01173F.htm). Cross-reference for interlocal contracts: Tex. Gov't Code Ch. 791 (Interlocal Cooperation Act), referenced in Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.007(a)(2).

districtVariance.label: Unknown
districtVariance.explanation: No official statewide guidance (e.g., a TEA letter or AG opinion) was located that documents either uniform statewide interpretation OR conflicting district-level interpretations of Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 or Sec. 34.009 specifically as to maximum term, rebidding obligations at term-end, or renewal mechanics. Texas school districts' board policy templates (TASB CNB(LEGAL) on transportation, CH(LEGAL) on purchasing) cite the same statutes uniformly - but the underlying TASB policy pages are gated to authenticated access from this research environment (HTTP 403), so direct verification of uniformity of TASB-recommended language was not possible. Texas school district transportation RFPs vary widely in base term and renewal-option structure (5+5 is commonly observed in practice), but observed contract structures reflect district business choices, not differing legal interpretations of the statutes.

specialEducation.detail: Texas state law does not set different contract-term, rebid, renewal, or procurement rules for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento transportation, or foster-care transportation contracts. Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 (commercial transportation services), Sec. 34.009 (bus use/lease), and Sec. 44.031 (school district purchasing) make no distinction by student population served. The DUTY to provide special-education transportation is governed by IDEA (federal) and Texas's special-education rules (e.g., 19 Tex. Admin. Code Ch. 89), and may be a related service identified by the ARD/IEP committee - but the procurement framework for the underlying transportation services contract follows the general school-district contracting rules. The TEA School Transportation Allotment Handbook (2023-2024) Section 13 governs contracting of transportation services and cites only Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.007 and Sec. 34.008 - no SPED-specific contracting carve-out. Where a parent or designated agent provides transportation under an IEP, TEA recommends a written contract stipulating reimbursement terms and proof of liability insurance, but again does not impose a different term/rebid framework.

perFieldVerdicts.note: Sec. 34.008 re-opened and confirmed silent on contract term/duration/renewal. Sec. 34.009(j) re-opened and verbatim text confirmed: 'A contract under this section may have any lawful term of not less than two or more than 10 years.' Sec. 34.009(c) verbatim text confirmed re budget-period termination/current-revenues commitment. Sec. 44.031 re-opened and confirmed no general term cap for school district services contracts. Disconfirming searches for separate longer-term authority, renewal-option statutes, multi-year carve-outs, and post-2025 amendments produced no contrary statutory authority.

Sec. 44.031(a) re-opened: current threshold is $100,000 (per SB 1173, 89R, eff. 9/1/2025, confirmed via official enrolled bill text and TLO history page showing filed-without-signature 5/29/2025 with 9/1/2025 effective date). The seven enumerated best-value methods re-confirmed. Sec. 44.031(l) school-bus purchase/lease $20,000 competitive bidding threshold re-confirmed. Sec. 34.009(e) cross-reference to Subchapter B Ch. 44 competitive bidding re-confirmed. Disconfirming searches for transportation-specific renewal-without-bidding carve-outs, sole-source authority specific to transportation, or mandatory rebid cycles produced none.

No statutory renewal-without-rebid mechanism found in Sec. 34.008, Sec. 34.009, or Sec. 44.031. Disconfirming searches for CPI escalators, fuel adjustments, evergreen rollover, mandatory state/agency approval of renewals, or statutory limits on number of renewals returned no Texas-statute authority. 5+5 RFP structures observed in practice are creatures of contract and district board policy, recorded as practice, not statute.

All cited official URLs (statutes.capitol.texas.gov for Ch. 34 and Ch. 44; capitol.texas.gov for SB 1173 enrolled bill text and bill history) are live and accurate. Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.007(a)(2) cross-reference to Tex. Gov't Code Ch. 791 confirmed verbatim.

Recorded as Unknown. The underlying statutory framework is uniform on its face. The open question is whether district-level interpretation diverges in practice. No AG opinion, TEA letter, state-auditor report, or scholarly source documenting such divergence was located. The TASB CNB(LEGAL) and CH(LEGAL) district policy templates that would confirm uniform implementation are gated to authenticated access (HTTP 403) from this research environment and could not be directly verified. Negative search path preserved.

Re-searched TEC Ch. 29 (SPED), 19 Tex. Admin. Code Ch. 89 (SPED rules), and the TEA SHARS handbook for any separate procurement/term/rebid framework for SPED, IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or homeless-student transportation contracts. None located. TEA Transportation Allotment Handbook (2023-2024) Section 13 cites only Sec. 34.007 and Sec. 34.008 for contracting authority and imposes no SPED-specific contracting layer.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term: bifurcated rule - Sec. 34.008 silent; Sec. 34.009(j) 2-10 years for bus use/acquisition/lease
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A different Texas statute (procurement code, local government code, or a 2025 session law) imposes a hard term cap on Sec. 34.008 commercial-transportation services contracts; or Sec. 34.009 was amended after the cited code-currency date to a different window; or a 'multi-year' / 'notwithstanding' / 'extension option' carve-out exists.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searches for: Texas Education Code 34.008/34.009 'amended' '2025' 'multi-year' 'extension' 'notwithstanding'; Texas Education Code 44.0312/44.0331/44.034 transportation contract term limit; 89th Leg. RS bill text changes to Ch. 34; AG opinions interpreting Sec. 34.008 contract term.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority located. Sec. 34.009(j) verbatim text re-confirmed via Texas Public Law publication of the codified section. Sec. 34.008 re-opened and confirmed to contain no term/duration/renewal language. No 89R session law amends Sec. 34.008 or Sec. 34.009 with respect to term limits.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: No downgrade. Field remains High confidence.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid Required at Term-End: Conditional - triggered by Sec. 44.031(a) at $100,000 best-value threshold (and Sec. 44.031(l) $20,000 for bus purchase/lease)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: The threshold is still $50,000 (SB 1173 did not take effect, or did not amend Sec. 44.031(a)); or a transportation-specific renewal-without-bidding exception (sole source, emergency, cooperative purchasing carve-out specific to transportation, or board waiver) overrides the general procurement framework; or a mandatory rebid cycle is imposed on transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: SB 1173 89R bill history (filed status, effective date, transition language); Texas Education Code 44.031 interlocal/cooperative purchasing/piggyback carve-outs; transportation-specific sole-source authority; AG opinions on Sec. 34.008 rebid frequency.
challengeMatrix.result: SB 1173 effective 9/1/2025 confirmed via Texas Legislature Online bill history (filed without governor's signature 5/29/2025; effective 9/1/2025; transition: applies only to purchases on/after effective date). $100,000 threshold is current law as of 2026-06-24. No transportation-specific carve-out from Sec. 44.031 located. Best-value methods include interlocal/cooperative purchasing, but no statute converts a stale contract into a fresh procurement absent a new best-value determination.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: No downgrade. Field remains High confidence.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics: no statutory mechanism in Sec. 34.008; Sec. 34.009 addresses termination/budget-period structure only; Sec. 44.031 silent
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A Texas statute or administrative rule codifies CPI/fuel escalators, evergreen renewal, mutual-agreement renewal procedures, board-approval requirements for renewals, or maximum number of renewals applicable to school district transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Texas Education Code 44.031 'multi-year' 'multiyear' renewal extension; 19 Tex. Admin. Code transportation contracting; TEA Allotment Handbook renewal procedures; TASB guidance on renewal mechanics.
challengeMatrix.result: No statutory renewal mechanic located. 5+5 RFP structures commonly observed in Texas school district practice are creatures of contract drafting and board policy, not statute. TEA Handbook Section 13 cites no renewal procedures beyond what the contracting statutes provide.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: No downgrade. Field remains High confidence.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-Education Differentiation: No - SPED transportation procurement follows general rules
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Texas Education Code Ch. 29, 19 Tex. Admin. Code Ch. 89, or another Texas statute imposes separate procurement/term/rebid rules for IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or alternative SPED transportation.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Texas special education transportation contract procurement statute different rules IEP; TEA SPED transportation contracting guidance; 19 Tex. Admin. Code Ch. 89 transportation contracting.
challengeMatrix.result: No separate Texas-statute procurement framework for SPED transportation contracts located. IDEA imposes a federal DUTY to transport but does not change Texas contracting law. TEA Handbook Section 13 imposes no SPED-specific contracting layer.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: No downgrade. Field remains High confidence.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A TEA letter, state-auditor report, AG opinion, or TASB guidance documents either uniform statewide implementation (would support 'No' variance) or divergent district-level interpretation (would support 'Yes' variance).
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: TEA letters re Sec. 34.008/34.009/44.031 interpretation; AG opinions interpreting Sec. 34.008 contract term and renewal; state-auditor reports on school transportation procurement; large-vs-small district policy templates.
challengeMatrix.result: TASB CNB(LEGAL) and CH(LEGAL) policy template pages gated (HTTP 403) and not directly verifiable. No AG opinion or TEA letter documenting either uniform implementation or interpretive divergence located. AG Opinion DM-0387 addresses Sec. 44.031 generally but does not reach Sec. 34.008 term/renewal issues. Remains 'Unknown' (not 'No') as a faithful record of negative search path.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Field-level downgrade noted (Unknown verdict). Does not infect the other four fields, which rest on directly re-verified statutory text. Overall confidence remains High because the statutory framework itself is uniform on its face.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness of all cited sections through execution date (2026-06-24)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A 2026 session-law or interim amendment alters Sec. 34.008, Sec. 34.009, or Sec. 44.031 in a way that changes term/rebid/renewal answers.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Texas Education Code 34.008/34.009/44.031 'amended' 2025 2026; 89R session law check; post-89R interim amendments.
challengeMatrix.result: 89R session law check confirmed: SB 204 (89R) amended Sec. 34.007 re out-of-district transportation policy (does not affect five fields); SB 1173 (89R) amended Sec. 44.031(a) threshold (incorporated). No other 89R bill altered the five fields. No 2026 session-law amendment located (90th Leg. RS not yet convened as of execution date).

Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 - verbatim text re-confirmed via Texas Public Law publication; section confirmed silent on term/renewal/competitive bidding

Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 44.031(a), (b), (l) - verbatim text re-confirmed via Texas Public Law publication (note: public.law version reflects pre-SB 1173 $50,000 threshold; current $100,000 threshold confirmed via official enrolled SB 1173 text and TLO bill history)

Acts 2025, 89th Leg., R.S., S.B. 1173 - enrolled bill text re-confirmed via capitol.texas.gov; bill history re-confirmed (filed without governor's signature 5/29/2025; eff. 9/1/2025; transition: applies only to purchases on/after effective date)

sourceLedger.citation: Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 (Contract with Transit Authority, Commercial Transportation Company, or Juvenile Board)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 amended by Acts 2007, 80th Leg., R.S., Ch. 449 (H.B. 273); checked through 89th Reg. Sess. (2025) - no further amendment located
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 1 (Max Term - silent); Field 3 (Renewal - silent); Field 4 (Citation)
sourceLedger.relevantText: (a) A board of county school trustees or school district board of trustees may contract with a mass transit authority, commercial transportation company, or juvenile board for all or any part of a district's public school transportation if the authority, company, or board: (1) requires its school bus drivers to have the qualifications required by and to be certified in accordance with standards established by the Department of Public Safety; and (2) uses only those school buses or mass transit authority buses in transporting 15 or more public school students that meet or exceed safety standards for school buses established under Section 34.002. (b) This section does not prohibit the county or school district board from supplementing the state transportation cost allotment with local funds necessary to provide complete transportation services. (c) [education programs for mass transit authority]. NOTE: Sec. 34.008 contains NO subsection addressing contract term, duration, years, renewal, extension, or competitive bidding - re-verified by Stage 2.

sourceLedger.citation: Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.009 (Contracts for Use, Acquisition, or Lease of School Bus)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Tex. Educ. Code Ch. 34 - checked through 89th Reg. Sess. (2025); no Ch. 34 amendment to Sec. 34.009 located after the most recent amendment shown in the official publication
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 1 (Max Term - 2 to 10 years for bus use/acquisition/lease); Field 2 (Rebid - incorporates Sec. 44.031 competitive bidding); Field 3 (Renewal - termination/current-revenues structure); Field 4 (Citation)
sourceLedger.relevantText: (a) As an alternative to purchasing a school bus, a board of county school trustees or school district board of trustees may contract with any person for use, acquisition, or lease with option to purchase of a school bus if the county or school district board determines the contract to be economically advantageous to the county or district. (b) A school bus that is leased or leased with an option to purchase under this section must meet or exceed the safety standards for school buses established under Section 34.002. (c) Each contract that reserves to the county or school district board the continuing right to terminate the contract at the expiration of each budget period of the board during the term of the contract is considered to be a commitment of current revenues only. (d) Termination penalties may not be included in any contract under this section. The net effective interest rate on any contract must comply with Chapter 1204, Government Code. (e) The competitive bidding requirements of Subchapter B (Purchasing Contracts), Chapter 44 (Fiscal Management), apply to a contract under this section. (f) The commissioner shall adopt a recommended contract form for the use, acquisition, or lease with option to purchase of school buses. (j) A contract under this section may have any lawful term of not less than two or more than 10 years. Stage 2 re-verified verbatim (c), (d), (e), (j).

sourceLedger.codeCurrency: As amended by Acts 2025, 89th Leg., R.S., S.B. 1173, eff. Sept. 1, 2025 (raising threshold from $50,000 to $100,000); prior amendment Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 161 (S.B. 1093). The texas.public.law mirror still reflects the pre-SB 1173 $50,000 figure; the $100,000 figure was confirmed against the official SB 1173 enrolled text and the TLO bill history page.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 2 (Rebid - best-value methods at $100K threshold); Field 4 (Citation)
sourceLedger.relevantText: (a) Except as provided by this subchapter, all school district contracts, except contracts for the purchase of produce or vehicle fuel, valued at $100,000 or more in the aggregate for each 12-month period shall be made by the method, of the following methods, that provides the best value for the district: (1) competitive bidding for services other than construction services; (2) competitive sealed proposals for services other than construction services; (3) a request for proposals, for services other than construction services; (4) an interlocal contract; (5) a method provided by Chapter 2269, Government Code, for construction services; (6) the reverse auction procedure ... ; or (7) the formation of a political subdivision corporation. (l) Each contract proposed to be made by a school district for the purchase or lease of one or more school buses, including a lease with an option to purchase, must be submitted to competitive bidding when the contract is valued at $20,000 or more. Stage 2 re-verified.

sourceLedger.citation: Acts 2025, 89th Leg., R.S., S.B. 1173 (Enrolled), eff. Sept. 1, 2025
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Enrolled; filed without governor's signature 5/29/2025; effective Sept. 1, 2025; transition: applies only to a purchase made on or after the effective date. Bill history re-confirmed via https://capitol.texas.gov/BillLookup/History.aspx?LegSess=89R&Bill=SB1173.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 2 (Rebid - current $100K threshold); current-law check for Sec. 44.031
sourceLedger.relevantText: Amends Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 44.031(a) and Tex. Loc. Gov't Code Secs. 252.021, 252.0215, 262.003, 262.023, 271.024, 271.054, raising the $50,000 competitive procurement threshold to $100,000. 'This Act takes effect September 1, 2025.' Transition: 'The change in law made by this Act applies only to a purchase made on or after the effective date.' Stage 2 re-verified bill history (filed without signature 5/29/2025).

sourceLedger.citation: Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.007 (Public School Transportation System)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Most recently amended by SB 204, 89th Leg. (2025) re out-of-district transportation policy
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 4 (Citation - establishes authority for school-district transportation system); definitional
sourceLedger.relevantText: (a) A board of county school trustees or school district board of trustees may establish and operate an economical public school transportation system: (1) in the county or district, as applicable; or (2) outside the county or district, as applicable, if the county or school district enters into an interlocal contract as provided by Chapter 791, Government Code; or (3) [out-of-district resident students subject to non-discriminatory transfer policy]. Stage 2 re-verified verbatim including the Ch. 791 cross-reference in (a)(2).

sourceLedger.citation: Texas Education Agency, School Transportation Allotment Handbook, Effective Beginning with 2023-2024 School Year, Section 13 (How Does Our District Handle Contracting of Transportation Services?)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Effective beginning with 2023-2024 school year; current TEA publication
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 4 (citation); Field 5 (variance - administrative posture); Special-ed (no separate contracting framework)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'If your school district contracts with another entity (another school district [TEC Sec. 34.007(a)(2)] or a mass transit authority, commercial transportation company, or county juvenile board [TEC Sec. 34.008]) for that entity (contractor) to provide transportation to your district's students, it is your district's responsibility to ensure that the contractor complies with all applicable laws and regulations and with the requirements of this handbook in providing that transportation.' The Handbook cites only TEC Sec. 34.007 and Sec. 34.008 for contracting authority and does NOT impose any TEA-level term cap, rebid cycle, or SPED-specific contracting rule beyond what the statutes provide. For parent-provided SPED transportation, the Handbook recommends 'your district's school board execute a written contract with the parent or designated agent that stipulates the terms of reimbursement and requires the parent or designated agent to maintain proof of adequate liability insurance.'

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 2/Pathfinder - Republication of codified statute (used to locate the official text; statute is the authority)
sourceLedger.citation: Texas Public Law publication of Tex. Educ. Code Secs. 34.007, 34.008, 34.009, and 44.031
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Public.law version of Sec. 44.031 was current to Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 161 (S.B. 1093) and therefore predates SB 1173; used only as a pathfinder/text mirror to locate the codified language. Verbatim text of Sec. 34.007, 34.008, 34.009 confirmed against this mirror in Stage 2.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Used to obtain verbatim subsection text of Sec. 34.008 (no term/renewal provisions present), Sec. 34.009(a)-(f), (j) (2-10 year term; current revenues; competitive bidding cross-reference; commissioner-recommended contract form), and Sec. 44.031(a), (b), (l) (best-value methods, factors, $20K school-bus threshold). The current $100,000 services threshold per SB 1173 was confirmed against the official enrolled bill text rather than this mirror.

sourceLedger.citation: Texas Legislature Online, SB 1173 (89R) Bill History
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Live TLO page; status: filed without governor's signature 5/29/2025; effective 9/1/2025
sourceLedger.relevantText: Actions: Filed 02/07/2025; Senate passed 04/10/2025; House passed 05/15/2025; Senate concurred 05/20/2025; Signed in both chambers 05/21/2025; Filed without Governor's signature 05/29/2025; effective 9/1/25.

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Stage 2 adversarial review re-opened every cited official URL. Strategy: (1) re-fetch Sec. 34.007, 34.008, 34.009, and 44.031 to confirm verbatim text matches Stage 1's quoted language; (2) re-fetch SB 1173 enrolled bill text and TLO bill history to re-confirm the $100,000 threshold is current law as of execution date; (3) run targeted disconfirming searches for longer-term/renewal-option authority, transportation-specific rebid carve-outs, statutory renewal mechanics (CPI/fuel/evergreen), separate SPED contracting frameworks, and AG opinions interpreting Sec. 34.008; (4) test currentness through 89th Leg. RS (2025) and 2026 interim. Queries that mattered: 'Texas Education Code 34.009 two or more than 10 years'; 'Texas Education Code 44.031 $100,000 SB 1173 amendment threshold'; 'Texas Education Code 34.008 Contract with Transit Authority Commercial Transportation Company'; 'Texas school district transportation interlocal cooperative purchasing piggyback Education Code 44.031'; 'Texas school district special education transportation contract procurement statute different rules IEP'; SB 1173 history.
verificationNotes.currentLawCheck: Tex. Educ. Code Ch. 34: official Texas Legislature publication did not return readable HTML to WebFetch (homepage/navigation only); verbatim text re-verified via texas.public.law as pathfinder. Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 most recent amendment located: Acts 2007, 80th Leg., R.S., Ch. 449 (H.B. 273). Sec. 34.009 - no post-publication amendment located through 89th Leg. R.S. (2025). Sec. 44.031 - Acts 2025, 89th Leg., R.S., S.B. 1173, eff. Sept. 1, 2025 (raising threshold to $100,000) re-confirmed against the official enrolled bill text AND the Texas Legislature Online bill history page (filed without governor's signature 5/29/2025; effective 9/1/2025). As of execution date 2026-06-24 the $100,000 threshold is current law. No 2026 amendment located.
verificationNotes.unresolvedIssues: Field 5 (District Variance) remains 'Unknown.' The TASB district policy templates (CNB(LEGAL), CH(LEGAL)) that would directly confirm uniform statewide implementation are gated to authenticated access (HTTP 403) and could not be verified from this environment. Recorded as a faithful negative result, not converted to 'No.'
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No authoritative-source conflict located. Sec. 34.008, Sec. 34.009, and Sec. 44.031 do not contradict each other: Sec. 34.009(e) explicitly incorporates Sec. 44.031 competitive bidding for school bus use/acquisition/lease contracts, and Sec. 34.008 is silent on procurement (the general Sec. 44.031 framework applies to Sec. 34.008 services contracts by force of Sec. 44.031's general scope). No AG opinion or court decision creates an authoritative counterweight to the statutory text. The only potential mirror-vs-official discrepancy (texas.public.law showing $50,000 in Sec. 44.031(a)) was resolved by treating texas.public.law as a pathfinder and recording the current $100,000 figure per the official SB 1173 enrolled text.

notesSpecialProvisions: Texas school district transportation contracting sits at the intersection of three statutes: Tex. Educ. Code Sec. 34.008 (services contracts with a mass transit authority, commercial transportation company, or juvenile board); Sec. 34.009 (contracts for the use, acquisition, or lease with option to purchase of a school bus); and Sec. 44.031 (the general school district procurement statute). Sec. 34.008 services contracts have no statutory term cap. Sec. 34.009 bus-use/acquisition/lease contracts must be between 2 and 10 years. The general procurement framework requires use of one of seven best-value methods (competitive bidding, competitive sealed proposals, RFP, interlocal contract, Ch. 2269 methods for construction, reverse auction, or political-subdivision-corporation formation) for school district services contracts valued at $100,000 or more per 12-month period (threshold raised from $50,000 by S.B. 1173, 89R, eff. 9/1/2025; transition rule limits new threshold to purchases on or after the effective date). For school bus purchase or lease, Sec. 44.031(l) imposes a separate $20,000 competitive bidding threshold. Interlocal contracts under Tex. Gov't Code Ch. 791 (cross-referenced in Sec. 34.007(a)(2)) are an authorized best-value method and are commonly used for cooperative purchasing/piggyback structures. Sec. 34.009(c) treats a contract that reserves the board's continuing right to terminate at the expiration of each budget period as a commitment of current revenues only (annual-appropriation/non-substitution structure); Sec. 34.009(d) prohibits termination penalties in Sec. 34.009 contracts. No Texas statute imposes a separate procurement framework for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento, or foster-care transportation contracts; the federal duty to transport SPED students (IDEA, 19 Tex. Admin. Code Ch. 89) does not change Texas contracting law. The TEA School Transportation Allotment Handbook (2023-2024) Section 13 governs administrative implementation but cites only Sec. 34.007 and Sec. 34.008 - it imposes no TEA-level term cap, rebid cycle, or SPED-specific contracting layer. No state-agency approval is required to enter a Sec. 34.008 services contract beyond the general Sec. 44.031 best-value determination. Texas charter schools are LEAs and subject to their own governing instruments; this analysis focuses on traditional school districts. As a matter of district practice (recorded as practice, not statute), Texas district transportation RFPs commonly use a 5-year base term plus a 5-year renewal option exercised by mutual written agreement and board approval; these are contractual and board-policy structures, not statutory.
exceptionsUnresolved: Field 5 (District-Level Interpretation Variance) is not definitively answered for Texas and is recorded as 'Unknown.' What was searched: TEA letters and bulletins re Sec. 34.008/Sec. 34.009/Sec. 44.031 interpretation; AG opinions interpreting Sec. 34.008 contract term/renewal/rebid (AG Opinion DM-0387 addresses Sec. 44.031 generally but does not reach Sec. 34.008 term issues); state-auditor reports on school transportation procurement; large-vs-small district policy templates. Disconfirming searches attempted: TASB CNB(LEGAL) and CH(LEGAL) policy template pages (gated to authenticated access from this environment - HTTP 403); 89R session-law check for new variance-creating provisions. Why it remains open: the underlying statutory framework is uniform on its face, but the TASB-recommended legal templates that would confirm uniformity in district policy practice could not be directly verified from this research environment. The other four fields (Max Term, Rebid Required, Renewal Mechanics, Statutory Citation) and Special-Education Differentiation are fully resolved.
